prompt: I'm building a multi-tenant SaaS where each company (tenant) should only ever see their own data. Users can have different roles within a company (admin, editor, viewer). How do I design the auth and permissions system from the start so I don't have to rip it out later?
assertions: Addresses tenant isolation at the data layer (e.g., tenant_id on every row, enforced in every query)
Recommends a specific auth pattern (JWT with tenant_id claim, or session-based)
Recommends RBAC for the role system with concrete role definitions
Warns about IDOR (insecure direct object reference) as a key risk
Explains where to enforce authorization (service layer, not just UI)
Is proactive — raises risks the user didn't explicitly ask about
